behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires testing that the data aggregation logic correctly combines results from three separate source modules (metrics, issues, deployments). The failure being caught—missing data from one or more sources—manifests only at the module-integration boundary, not within a single function. Mocking the three sources is appropriate since we're testing the aggregator's coordination logic, not the sources themselves.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the three data sources (metrics API, issue tracker, deployment log) with distinct test data. Call the data aggregation function. Verify that the aggregated output contains key data from all three sources—e.g., metrics count > 0, issues list is populated, deployments list contains expected items.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This behavior tests error handling and resilience across module boundaries—when a source fails, the data collector must handle the exception gracefully and communicate the failure (via the unavailable note) through to the report generator. The failure only manifests at the interaction between collector and sources; testing isolated exception handling in a single function would miss boundary-level issues.
behaviors.test_description: Configure two data sources to return normal data and one source to raise a network exception. Call the data aggregation function. Verify that the output includes a 'data unavailable' note for the failed source while successfully including data from the other two sources.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Testing the accuracy of an LLM-generated summary requires calling the real LLM; summary quality is non-deterministic and varies run-to-run. Mocking the LLM would never catch failures in how accurately the real model represents input data—a mocked output is definitionally 'accurate.' The failure being caught is real model behavior, not integration logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the summarization function with structured test data containing specific, verifiable key metrics (e.g., '5 deployments', '3 critical issues', '98% uptime'). Use the real LLM to generate a narrative summary. Verify that the generated summary accurately represents these values (e.g., deployment count mentioned, severity of issues reflected, uptime metric present).

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: This end-to-end behavior requires multiple real infrastructure elements in sequence: real LLM summarization (agentic step with non-deterministic output), real email delivery (system-level network/SMTP), and their interaction. Testing the complete pipeline requires the real LLM to generate report content and real email infrastructure to verify inbox delivery. Mocking either would hide real failures (LLM quality, delivery infrastructure).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the complete end-to-end workflow: trigger data collection from configured internal APIs, call the real LLM to generate a narrative summary, configure real or test-harness email delivery to a test inbox, and verify that the generated report is successfully delivered to the recipient's inbox within the expected timeframe.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real operating system file I/O to verify that reports are correctly persisted to the filesystem with proper permissions and content integrity, and that they can be retrieved later. Mocking file operations would hide real failures: permission errors, disk space exhaustion, filesystem permission issues, path validation problems. The scheduled execution component also requires real OS scheduling behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure email delivery to point to an unreachable server. Execute the report generation. Verify that the generated report is written to the configured local fallback file with correct content and proper file permissions. Restore email connectivity, simulate the next scheduled run by invoking the retry mechanism, and verify that the saved report is successfully delivered to the email recipient.
